passed_ownership_repairs: stop --json refuses probable-owned runs without stopping the fixture process
stop --json refuses unknown ownership without stopping the fixture process
stop --json refuses matching pid and listener without trusted spawn proof
stop --json refuses an external process without stopping the external listener
stop --json preserves stop_failed evidence when termination does not complete
managed CLI start/stop ownership flows outside T024 output residuals

remaining_failures.test: stop --json stops a verified-owned run and records stopped evidence
remaining_failures.actual: Stop succeeds and releases the process, but JSON shape returns data.process instead of data.stopped[]/data.failed[].

remaining_failures.test: force-stop --json preserves the verified ownership gate
remaining_failures.actual: CLI route returns unknown_command for force-stop.

remaining_failures.test: restart --json stops, waits for port release, and starts a new run in one transaction
remaining_failures.actual: Restart succeeds, but JSON shape returns readiness/process instead of stoppedRun/startedRun/transactionId.

remaining_failures.test: restart --json records port_release_timeout when the stopped task leaves its port occupied
remaining_failures.actual: CLI output/error mapping reports partial_failure instead of port_release_timeout.

concerns: No CLI/output files were edited, so the four remaining lifecycle failures are preserved for T024.
The workspace is nested under a broader git root where launchdeck appears untracked, so git diff is not available for this project subtree.
recovery_hints: Runtime stop/restart no longer upgrades PID/port/local-state-only evidence to verified-owned.
A Launchdeck-started process receives a source=launchdeck-spawn ownership proof at spawn persistence time; existing processes do not receive this proof.

Stop accepts current proveRunOwnership verified-owned evidence, or a validated launchdeck-spawn proof when current evidence is unknown/probable and not contradicted by listener mismatch or dead process evidence.
Regression coverage: test/lifecycle-ownership.test.js includes a matching PID/listener stale-state case with no trusted spawn proof and asserts stop refuses without killing the listener.
